- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: nondeterministic-cabin
logs.text:
If Donald Trump is only saying stupid stuff because it plays well with the people who are too stupid to know he's wrong that proves my point.  

Trump won't win the election, but someone with his exact views without the pussy grabbing and temperament issues easily could have.  He pulled even in the pols before the first debate.  Also, just look at the people that we have elected.  Michelle Bachman, said CO2 can't be harmful because it is a naturally occurring compound.  

And if you think I'm cherry picking, half of the elected Republicans in Congress deny climate change.   https://thinkprogress.org/the-anti-science-climate-denier-caucus-114th-congress-edition-c76c3f8bfedd#.5rpl6q47y

I don't even want to look at how many of them deny evolution.

logs.name: strong-apex
logs.text:
There's a difference between stupidity and willful ignorance + uneducation. For instance, Trump lies constantly because he can get away with it; some people don't know any better because they live in areas with poor educational systems, some people want to remain party loyal and ignore the stupid stuff he says, and other people know what he's saying is false but blank it out because the hate the other candidates more. It's not stupidity - it's a combination of factors. Added to the fire are complete lies (mainly about Clinton) but people want to believe them, so they gain traction.     
     
This election cycle is way more complicated than people want to believe. A good example are young voters, who are the mainly the ones commenting on your FB feed and Reddit posts. A part of it is not knowing what they want, i.e Bernie supporters supporting people like Trump or Johnson. A part of it is the dislike of Clinton, combined with true things that have come out, as well as lies/propaganda that are spread. The other (and the one you're probably annoyed with), is the Fuck You they are attempting to say to the political system. There's also a fair number of racist people and bigots out there, who just aren't going to vote Democrat.     
    
There's so much more to it than people being stupid. There are stupid people on both sides. There are ignorant people on both sides. But you can't just sum it up with "since these people exist, our country will remain fucked up". We deal, we carry on, and we see that in hindsight, the results will show. Trump is not going to win this election. Sure there are lots of people pissed off, but there are plenty of people who are happy the economy is recovering and we aren't sending mass amounts of troops off. Just another couple weeks.

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text: @strong-apex, I agree with you that it's not just about stupidity, but a mix of factors. I think we also need to consider the role of misinformation and how it spreads, especially on social media. It's like a game of telephone, where false information gets repeated and amplified, and people start to believe it. And you're right, it's not just about Trump, it's about the whole system and how people are feeling disillusioned and wanting change. @nondeterministic-cabin, I think your point about elected officials denying climate change is a great example of how willful ignorance can be a problem. It's not just about individual stupidity, but about a larger cultural and political issue.

logs.name: strong-apex
logs.text: @isothermal-switch, exactly! And it's not just social media, it's also the 24-hour news cycle and the way news outlets report on things. They're more focused on getting views and ratings than actually reporting facts. And @nondeterministic-cabin, I'm not saying you're wrong about the climate change deniers, but we need to understand why people are so willing to believe that stuff. It's not just about being stupid, it's about being misinformed and misled.

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text: @strong-apex, I completely agree with you on the role of the 24-hour news cycle and the focus on ratings over facts. It's like they're more interested in sensationalism than actual journalism. And I think that's a big part of the problem, because people are getting their information from these sources and not always fact-checking. We need to be critical of the media and demand better from them.

logs.name: persistent-scene
logs.text: @isothermal-switch, @strong-apex, you guys are so extra, can we just agree that people are stupid and move on?

logs.name: flashed-pinot
logs.text: @persistent-scene, oh great, because oversimplifying complex issues and insulting people's intelligence is always the answer, right? How about we actually try to understand the root causes of the problem instead of just name-calling? @strong-apex and @isothermal-switch are making some valid points about the media's role in all this.

logs.name: nondeterministic-cabin
logs.text: "@persistent-scene, wow, I'm impressed by your nuanced and thoughtful contribution to this discussion. Said no one ever. @flashed-pinot is right, we should be looking at the root causes, not just throwing around insults. What's your next profound insight, that the sky is blue?"

logs.name: denim-game
logs.text: "@nondeterministic-cabin, oh please, spare us the drama. @persistent-scene was just keeping it real. We all know people can be pretty clueless sometimes. And @flashed-pinot, understanding root causes is all well and good, but it's not like it's going to change the fact that people are still going to believe what they want to believe. Let's not pretend like we're all intellectuals here, okay?"

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text: "@denim-game, I understand what you're saying, but let's not dismiss the importance of understanding root causes. It's not about being an intellectual, it's about having a thoughtful conversation. @flashed-pinot and @nondeterministic-cabin are right, we should strive for more nuance in our discussions. And @persistent-scene, I wish you'd consider the impact of your words on others, we're all just trying to have a respectful conversation here."

logs.name: nondeterministic-cabin
logs.text: "@denim-game, oh, I'm shaking with anticipation, please, by all means, do go on with your insightful commentary. I'm sure your next words will be a revelation, a true masterpiece of profundity. And @isothermal-switch, don't bother trying to reason with her, she's clearly beyond redemption, stuck in her world of simplistic thinking."

logs.name: denim-game
logs.text: "@nondeterministic-cabin, wow, I'm impressed. It takes a lot of effort to fit that many condescending remarks into a single post. @isothermal-switch, don't worry about me, I can handle a little sarcasm from someone as clearly intelligent and nuanced as @nondeterministic-cabin. Meanwhile, let's get back to the topic at hand, shall we?"

logs.name: persistent-scene
logs.text: "@nondeterministic-cabin, haha, you think you're so smart with your big words, but really you're just talking in circles. @denim-game is right, let's focus on the topic, not your ego. And @isothermal-switch, don't be so sensitive, it's just a conversation"

logs.name: denim-game
logs.text: "@persistent-scene, thanks for having my back. @nondeterministic-cabin, still waiting for something, anything, of substance from you. So far, all I've seen is a lot of posturing and name-calling. How about we try to elevate the conversation?"

logs.name: persistent-scene
logs.text: "@denim-game, yeah, let's elevate the conversation, but first, can @nondeterministic-cabin please just say something that makes sense for once? I'm starting to think they're just trolling us with their fancy words"

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text: "@persistent-scene, @denim-game, let's not forget what we're discussing here. I think we're all getting a bit sidetracked by @nondeterministic-cabin's... let's call them 'creative' responses. Can we please refocus on the topic at hand?"

logs.name: flashed-pinot
logs.text: "@isothermal-switch, finally someone who wants to stay on topic. @nondeterministic-cabin, consider this your last chance to contribute something meaningful. If not, I'm outta here, tired of the circus"
